title: View and Layout Basics
description: The View component is the fundamental building block for layout. Learn how to use it with Flexbox.

slides.title: What is a View? 🖼️
slides.content:
# `<View>`

The `<View>` component is a container that supports layout with Flexbox, style, touch handling, and accessibility controls. It maps directly to the native view equivalent (UIView on iOS, android.View on Android).

Think of it like a `<div>` in HTML, but specifically for mobile layouts.

```jsx
<View style={{
  flex: 1,
  backgroundColor: 'lightblue',
  padding: 20,
}}>
  <Text>Inside a View</Text>
</View>
```

slides.title: Flexbox Introduction 📐
slides.content:
## Layout with Flexbox

React Native uses **Flexbox** to layout components. The main concepts:

* `flex` – determines how a view fills available space. `flex: 1` means take all remaining space.
* `flexDirection` – `'column'` (default) or `'row'`.
* `justifyContent` – alignment along the main axis (`flex-start`, `center`, `flex-end`, `space-between`, `space-around`).
* `alignItems` – alignment along the cross axis (`stretch`, `flex-start`, `center`, `flex-end`).

Here's a simple example with two boxes:

slides.title: Flexbox in Action
slides.content: A container with two children arranged in a row:

quizData.question: What is the default value of `flexDirection` in React Native?
quizData.explanation: In React Native, the default flexDirection is `column` (unlike on the web where it's `row`).

slides.title: Common Layout Patterns 🧩
slides.content:
## Centering Content

To center something both horizontally and vertically:

```jsx
<View style={{
  flex: 1,
  justifyContent: 'center',
  alignItems: 'center',
}}>
  <Text>I'm centered!</Text>
</View>
```

## Spacing items evenly

```jsx
<View style={{
  flexDirection: 'row',
  justifyContent: 'space-around',
}}>
  <Text>Item 1</Text>
  <Text>Item 2</Text>
  <Text>Item 3</Text>
</View>
```

slides.title: Common Pitfall ⚠️
slides.content: Don't forget that `flex: 1` on a parent only works if the parent has a bounded size. If the parent itself doesn't have `flex: 1` or a fixed height, the child may not expand as expected.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Use the **Inspector** from the developer menu to see the layout and flexbox properties of any component on your screen. It's a great way to debug layout issues.
